items.title: One Million Passports Leaked Online
items.summary: A database of almost a million passports from around the world was leaked online. Note what happened. A high-value credential a passport was used in an ancillary low-value authentication system: ID verification for cannabis dispensaries. And it s the low-value system that got hacked, putting the high-value credential at risk.

items.title: Interesting Paper Exploring Prompt Injection
items.summary: This is a fascinating explotation of how LLMs fall for prompt injection attacks. It turns out that they learn to recognize the style of text in different role/instruction blocks, and not just the tags. Their conclusion: Role tags were a formatting trick that became the security architecture and the cognitive scaffolding of modern LLMs. We ve shown …
